title: GAIA Review System v2 — rubric file schema (E68-S2)
description: JSON Schema (draft-07) for individual rubric files consumed by the layered rubric loader (rubric-loader.sh) and the deterministic merger (rubric-merger.sh). Each rubric is a single layer in the four-layer composition: base + regimes (in declaration order) + optional domain + optional project (per ADR-079, RFC 7396 JSON-merge-patch). NFR-RSV2-4 and NFR-RSV2-10 require schema-validation discipline and byte-identical merger output.

schema_version.description: Rubric schema version. SemVer-style major.minor (e.g., '1.0', '1.1'). Loader rejects rubrics whose major version does not match the loader's supported major (currently '1').

skill.description: Review skill this rubric applies to. One of the six base review skills (code, qa, test, security, perf, a11y) or a forward-compatible extension (e.g., 'mobile' for E74).

severity_rules.description: Ordered list of rules. Each rule maps a category/pattern to a severity verdict. The list IS replaced (not concatenated) when a later layer overrides earlier ones — RFC 7396 array-replace semantics, deliberate so regimes can omit base entries.

extends.description: Optional declarative reference to another rubric the loader should layer beneath this one. Informational only at this version — the loader resolves layering from project-config.yaml, not from this field.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata (author, date, regime ID, references). Not consumed by the merger; preserved verbatim through layers.

id.description: Stable rule identifier — unique within the rubric. Convention: '<skill>.<short-name>' (e.g., 'code.unbounded-loop').

category.description: Category bucket — used by the verdict-resolver to group findings (e.g., 'security', 'performance', 'accessibility', 'data-handling').

pattern.description: Pattern or signature the deterministic evidence collector matches against (regex, AST query, or natural-language descriptor consumed by the LLM). Format is skill-specific.

severity.description: Severity verdict. One of Critical|High|Medium|Low|Info — aligned with the verdict-resolver precedence (FR-RSV2-22 / NFR-RSV2-4).

description.description: Human-readable rationale — what the rule detects and why it matters.

examples.description: Optional list of concrete example snippets that would (or would not) trigger the rule.

remediation.description: Optional suggested fix or mitigation guidance.

references.description: Optional list of references (CWE IDs, OWASP IDs, ADR anchors, RFC numbers).
